title: About the Team
About the Team
About the Team
text:
About This Project
This research is part of AD688 at Boston University, analyzing gender disparities in hiring and political influence on employment patterns using 2024 Lightcast job market data.

title: ETL Pipeline — Master Orchestrator
text:
Runs the full extract → transform → load pipeline. Add new datasets by repeating the three-step pattern below.

import subprocess, sys# Run each ETL step as a notebook so outputs are preservedsubprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "jupyter", "nbconvert", "--to", "notebook",                "--execute", "01_extract.ipynb", "--output", "01_extract.ipynb"], check=True)subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "jupyter", "nbconvert", "--to", "notebook",                "--execute", "02_transform.ipynb", "--output", "02_transform.ipynb"], check=True)subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "jupyter", "nbconvert", "--to", "notebook",                "--execute", "03_load.ipynb", "--output", "03_load.ipynb"], check=True)print("Pipeline complete.")

title: ETL Step 1 — Extract
text:
Defines extract(filepath) — loads a CSV, prints a full inspection report, and returns a DataFrame.
import pandas as pd

def extract(filepath):
    df = pd.read_csv(filepath, low_memory=False)

    pd.set_option('display.max_columns', None)

    print("Shape:", df.shape)
    print("\nColumns:", df.columns.tolist())
    print("\nDtypes:\n", df.dtypes)
    print("\nHead:")
    display(df.head(5))
    print("\nTail:")
    display(df.tail(5))
    print("\nMissing values:\n", df.isnull().sum())
    print("\nBasic stats:")
    display(df.describe())

    return df

title: ETL Step 1 — Extract
text:
Run extract on Lightcast dataset

df = extract("../data/raw/lightcast_2024.csv")

title: Gender Disparities in Hiring & Political Influence
text:
This project examines gender based disparities in hiring patterns across industries and explores how political influence measured by state political lean (red vs. blue) shapes employment outcomes for men and women in 2024.
Using the Lightcast dataset as our baseline, we apply a structured ETL pipeline to extract, clean, and analyze real world job market data, publishing our findings as an evolving research portfolio.

This project examines gender based disparities in hiring patterns across industries and explores how political influence measured by state political lean (red vs. blue) shapes employment outcomes for men and women in 2024.
Using the Lightcast dataset as our baseline, we apply a structured ETL pipeline to extract, clean, and analyze real world job market data, publishing our findings as an evolving research portfolio.

title: Gender Disparities in Hiring & Political Influence
text:
Research Questions

How do hiring patterns differ for men vs. women across industries?
Do gender based employment disparities vary between red and blue states?

title: ETL Step 3 — Load
text:
Defines load(df, output_path) — saves the cleaned DataFrame to data/processed/ as a CSV.

import os

def load(df, output_path):
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(output_path), exist_ok=True)
    df.to_csv(output_path, index=False)
    print(f"Saved {len(df)} rows to {output_path}")

title: ETL Step 2 — Transform
text:
Defines transform(df) — selects relevant columns, cleans nulls, standardizes types, and merges red/blue state classification. Returns df_clean.

import pandas as pd

def transform(df):
    # Select columns relevant to gender & political analysis
    keep_cols = [
        'TITLE_NAME',
        'STATE', 'STATE_NAME',
        'NAICS2_NAME',
        'SALARY_FROM', 'SALARY_TO',
        'EMPLOYMENT_TYPE_NAME',
        'REMOTE_TYPE_NAME',
        'SOC_2021_2', 'SOC_2021_2_NAME',
        'MIN_EDULEVELS_NAME',
        'MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE'
    ]
    df_clean = df[keep_cols].copy()

    # Drop rows missing state (needed for red/blue join)
    df_clean = df_clean.dropna(subset=['STATE'])

    # Standardize state to uppercase string
    df_clean['STATE'] = df_clean['STATE'].astype(str).str.upper().str.strip()

    print("Shape after transform:", df_clean.shape)
    print("\nMissing values:\n", df_clean.isnull().sum())

    return df_clean
